economic_notes: GBE financial information not available and not consolidated. Tsetspa7 Forestry Limited Partnership (19.59% interest) reported revenue of $17,472,060 for 2024. Three internally restricted capital bylaw funds established for infrastructure, capital, and economic development totalling $4,372,643 at fair market value. Entities include TTQ Economic Development Corporation (100%), Takem Ti Qwelsa7 Eco-Resource Corporation (100%), Xa'xtsa Gas Station Ltd. (100%), Tsetspa7 Forestry Limited Partnership (19.59%), Tsetspa7 Loakam (Logging) Ltd., and 1020376 B.C. Ltd. (33.33%).
notes: Audit is qualified on two bases: (1) funded tangible capital assets including roads, bridges, buildings, water and utility systems not capitalized in prior years; (2) GBE financial information not available and not accounted for in consolidated statements. Long-term debt total of $4,473,325 includes long-term debt ($269,470), demand loans ($1,532,056), capital lease obligations ($671,683), and CMHC mortgages ($2,000,116). Annual debt service calculated as principal repayments: long-term debt $102,936 + CMHC mortgage $118,755 + demand loan $143,757 + capital lease $44,011 = $409,459. OSR calculated as total revenue $9,022,133 minus ISC $3,484,026 minus Province of BC $2,029,257 minus FNHA $1,016,696 minus CMHC $138,421 minus FNESC $190,409 minus Department of Natural Resources $107,410 = approximately $2,056,914; however using total government transfers of ISC+Province+FNHA+CMHC+DNR = $6,775,809 gives OSR of $2,246,324. Remuneration schedule not included in provided text.
warnings: Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
